summary: The sync-react workflow failed when attempting to push a branch to the Next.js repository. The git push command was rejected with a 403 Forbidden error due to the nextjs-bot user lacking push permissions.
root_cause: The GitHub Actions bot user (nextjs-bot) does not have permission to push to the vercel/next.js repository. The git push to 'origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422' failed with HTTP 403 (Forbidden).

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicitly states the bot user does not have permission to push to the repository

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: HTTP 403 Forbidden indicates an authentication or authorization failure

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: The git push command failed with exit code 128, confirming the push operation did not complete
Pinpoints the exact location in the script where the failure occurred

suggested_fix: Verify that the GitHub Actions token or bot authentication credentials have write/push permissions to the vercel/next.js repository. Check the workflow's token configuration and ensure the bot user (nextjs-bot) has been granted the necessary repository permissions.
